title: paspalum setaceum var. muhlenbergii
intro: Paspalum setaceum var. muhlenbergii, also known as Munro's grass, is a type of ornamental grass native to the southern United States and the Caribbean. It is a perennial grass that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, with its health benefits attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Paspalum setaceum var. muhlenbergii include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water to utilize its medicinal properties.
